instruction: Go straight past the pool. Walk between the bar and chairs.  Stop when you get to the corner of the bar.  That's where you will wait.

text: I'm looking around this narrow corridor with wooden slat walls, trying to get my bearings since I can't clearly make out a pool or bar from here.
Looking at the spatial layout, I'm oriented away from the pool with it to my left, and there's a bar or lounge area behind me. I need to turn around and head toward that room.
Turning around to face the opposite direction, I should now be looking at the room with the chairs and bar setup.

I'm noticing the room layout with a bar counter ahead and analyzing the clearance distances—the left side is blocked by a wall, center is tight around a corner, but the right opens up significantly. I should angle right to follow the open path and move forward into the room.

I can see the layout now—pool on the right, bar counter with shelves on the left, and chairs in the middle near the loungers. I need to walk forward straight through this space, keeping the pool to my right and the bar and chairs to my left, until I reach the corner where the counter ends.
I'm moving forward through the space, tracking the bar counter along the left wall as it approaches a corner where dark wooden doors come into view—likely where the counter meets the wall.
I've moved past the bar counter and it's now out of view behind me on the left. Looking around, I see chairs and lounge seating, but I'm not sure if I've gone too far around the corner.

I can see the bar counter is positioned at the 270° angle, with the TV, shelves, and drinks setup. The counter appears to end near a doorway with steps leading to another room. I need to turn left to face that direction and navigate toward the corner where the counter meets the steps.

To get there, I'll turn right 90° (which is equivalent to turning left 270°) to face that bar area, then move toward the corner.

I see the issue now—the clearance values match the "right" view from my earlier look around, which means turning right 90° physically corresponds to what was labeled as the "right" direction. So the "left" labeled view must require turning left 90° instead. I'm overcomplicating this. Since I'm currently at +90° right from my starting position, and I need to reach the "left" view which empirically requires turning left 90° from the original heading, I should turn left 180° from where I am now to get there.
The clearance measurements match the left label, so I'm now oriented toward the bar counter.

I'm orienting myself to the space now—the bar counter runs from the left side near the steps to the right where it meets a dark wood cabinet, and the corner I need to reach is that near end by the steps and doorway.

I'm noticing the bar counter's corner on the right side of the frame, about 1.4 meters away near the steps. That front corner might already be my target, so I should turn right slightly to center it in my view.

I'm noticing this position matches what I saw earlier with the bar counter ahead and that 1.8 meter clearance in the center. It's the same spot as step 109, so I should move toward the near corner of the bar that was on the left side by the steps based on my earlier observation.

I'm looking at the same view and identifying the front-left corner of the bar counter—where it meets the stairs—as the landmark to stop at. That corner is already fairly close, maybe a meter or two away diagonally ahead and to my left.

I'll move forward a couple of steps and angle slightly left to reach that corner, then stop there.

I'm at the corner of the bar counter now, with about 1.2 meters of clearance to the shelf on the right—that's close enough to the target. I've confirmed I've moved past the pool and navigated between the bar and chairs, so this corner position works as a stopping point.

I have stopped the episode at the corner of the bar counter, adjacent to the steps and shelving unit — this is where the bar counter meets the wall/cabinet, satisfying the instruction's endpoint ("the corner of the bar") after passing the pool and walking between the bar and the chairs.
